mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 20-day forward returns when PAYEMS growth momentum is positive.
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with labor market healing
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising month-over-month, the Fed is in active tightening mode. This contractionary monetary stance increases discount rates and borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as equity valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% and CPIAUCSL at 320.580 as of 2013-09
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation
failure_condition: When trigger is active, SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,200 thousand units indicate strong residential construction demand and consumer confidence. This signals healthy aggregate demand and forward earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
regime_hint: post-crisis housing recovery with improving demand
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1200, SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below its 3-month moving average while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling weakening supply-side capacity utilization and labor market softening. This joint deterioration predicts negative 20-day forward returns as the market reprices recession risk upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 3-month MA AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.88 and UNRATE at 4.2% as of 2013-08
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with supply constraints tightening
failure_condition: When trigger is active, SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) exhibit month-over-month contraction, indicating weakening consumer spending and aggregate demand. This demand destruction signal predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings guidance is revised downward and equity risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: When RSAFS contracts, SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 4.5% while payroll growth (PAYEMS) accelerates above 200,000 persons monthly, indicating a tight labor market with wage pressure building. This dual tightening in labor supply predicts positive 20-day forward returns as the market prices in higher nominal earnings growth and inflation expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% and PAYEMS at 159561 thousand as of 2013-09
regime_hint: tight labor market with full employment approaching
failure_condition: When trigger is active, SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, indicating accommodative monetary policy supporting strong demand. This growth-supportive regime produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity risk premiums compress and earnings growth accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23528.047 billion as of 2013-05, FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of 2013-09
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy supporting above-trend growth
failure_condition: When trigger is active, SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) exhibits sustained month-over-month growth above 0.3% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable or declining, signaling healthy supply-side expansion without demand-pull inflation. This benign supply growth regime produces positive 5-day forward returns as the market reprices stagflation risk downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0.3% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month MA change < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.88 and CPIAUCSL at 320.580 as of 2013-08
regime_hint: non-inflationary supply expansion with productivity gains
failure_condition: When trigger is active, SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
